description_long: This is a vintage Ricoh KR-5 single-lens reflex (SLR) camera, featuring a robust black body with metallic accents and a prominent lens assembly. The camera has a textured grip surface, a large lens barrel with visible focus and aperture markings, and a lens cap attached. The top of the camera displays a hot shoe mount, shutter speed dial, and film advance lever, along with the Ricoh branding and model number. The back of the camera is relatively plain, with a viewfinder and a film door release. The overall design is boxy and utilitarian, typical of analog film cameras from the late 20th century, and it appears to be constructed primarily from metal and plastic components.
description: A vintage Ricoh KR-5 SLR camera with a black, boxy body, metallic details, and a large lens with visible markings. The camera features a textured grip, prominent branding, and classic analog controls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Ricoh branding, lens with cap, and control dials on top.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the lens barrel, grip, and part of the top controls.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back, viewfinder, and film door area.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view showing the lens, grip, and side controls with the KR-5 model marking.
